sections.paragraph:
an unused root, which seems to have had the power to be or stand erect (perhaps kindred to אָמַר, for verbs פא֞ and פת֞ are often of the same power, especially in Arabic). Arab. تمر Conj. XI. riguit hasta, membrum virile; تَمَرُ palm-tree, تَامُورُ, and تَامُورَةُ a tower. As to the usage of the Talmudists, see תִּימָרָה .

Derivatives, תָּמָר-תִּמֹּורָה, תִּימָרָה, תַּמְרוּר No. II., compare תַּדְמֹר.
